meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Students: Master Testing Workflows
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no testing knowledge and professional SaaS pricing. Learn to build resilient apps using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of testing knowledge while implementing SaaS pricing, focus on contract-based development. Use Claude Code to generate unit tests alongside feature logic, then leverage Antigravity to simulate edge-case pricing scenarios. This dual-tool approach ensures your revenue logic is mathematically sound before you ever deploy to production.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank IDE, the architecture of your first SaaS pricing model looming over you like a mountain. You know the features you want to build, but the terrifying void of 'no testing knowledge' keeps you from pushing to production. You fear that one wrong calculation in your subscription logic will break your users' trust before you even launch.

But here is the secret: you don't need to be a QA engineer to build robust software. By integrating automated testing directly into your development loop, you transform your lack of experience into a competitive advantage. Let's turn that anxiety into an automated, bulletproof pricing engine that handles edge cases like a senior architect.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, map out your tiers using a structured schema to ensure consistency across the application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model supporting monthly/annual cycles, discount coupons, and tiered feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context 'Define pricing schema in src/types/pricing.ts'

steps.title: Generate Test-Driven Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to create the pricing calculator with built-in unit tests, ensuring the math is verifiable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pricing calculator function that calculates prorated charges for mid-cycle upgrades, including unit tests for edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/utils/calculator.ts with Jest tests for prorated billing'

steps.title: Simulate Revenue Scenarios
steps.description: Use Antigravity to run simulations against your calculator to identify potential financial leaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the pricing calculator for off-by-one errors in leap years or subscription end-dates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute simulation suite against calculator logic'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Secure your billing by testing webhook payloads against your local pricing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock Stripe webhook handler that validates incoming subscription events against our pricing schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context 'Implement webhook handler with validation'

steps.title: Automate Regression Testing
steps.description: Lock in your progress by automating the test suite to run on every commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs our pricing unit tests and simulation suite on every push.

pro_tips: Always test the 'null' state: Ensure your pricing logic gracefully handles users with no active subscription without throwing 500 errors.
Mock your time: Use libraries like Jest-date-mock to simulate end-of-month billing cycles, which are common points of failure.
Prioritize contract testing: If your frontend and backend share the same pricing interface, you eliminate 80% of integration bugs.
Log the 'Why', not just the 'What': Include transaction context in your logs to make auditing payments easier for your future self.
Start with the 'Free' tier edge case: Most bugs occur when transitioning from free to paid or when a trial expires.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing logic is tested, deploy to a staging environment and use a tool like Postman to run your suite against live API endpoints.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build enterprise-grade billing systems that scale. Stop fearing the code and start mastering the architecture. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
